message:
refactor(storage): replace writable_schema FTS cleanup with DROP TABLE + add duplicate schema regression test

The previous FTS schema cleanup in both materialize_fresh_fts_schema_via_rusqlite()
and rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite() used PRAGMA writable_schema to manually delete
sqlite_master rows matching 'fts_messages%'. This approach was fragile: it
required a separate connection for the cleanup phase, had to toggle
writable_schema ON/OFF, and could miss shadow table rows if naming conventions
changed.

Replace with a clean DROP TABLE IF EXISTS pattern via a shared helper
drop_fts_schema_via_rusqlite() that properly cascades to FTS5 shadow tables.
This eliminates the need for a separate pre-cleanup connection open/close cycle
in both functions, reducing total rusqlite connection opens from 2 to 1 per call.

Also adds a targeted regression test (rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite_cleans_duplicate_legacy_schema_rows)
that reproduces the duplicate sqlite_master row scenario that motivated the
original writable_schema approach, verifying the DROP TABLE strategy correctly
handles legacy databases with corrupted schema metadata while preserving FTS
search functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
